name: Lotus
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Lotus (Christian Freeling, 1980s) — a Go-like territory game on the 72 vertices of the Kensington rhombitrihexagonal board. Bi-coloured flip stones: a captured group is not removed but REVERSED to the capturer's colour (with a cascading re-reversal if the new group is itself liberty-less). Suicide is legal, holding a 'lotus' (all 6 points of a hexagon) grants permanent immunity, and passing nudges a marker that adds bonus points at the end. Stones only added or flipped, so the game always terminates. Highest territory wins.
